The Mummy (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $80,101,125Details
International Box Office $329,852,780
Worldwide Box Office $409,953,905
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,109,132
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $10,155,460
Total $17,264,592
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Thought safely entombed in a tomb deep beneath the unforgiving desert, an ancient princess whose destiny was unjustly taken from her is awakened in our current day, bringing with her malevolence grown over millennia and terrors that defy human comprehension.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$31,668,375 (39.5% of total gross)
Legs:2.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:19.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$195,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,035 opening theaters/4,035 max. theaters, 3.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $106,801,496

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 9th, 2017 (Wide) by Universal
June 9th, 2017 (IMAX) by Universal
International Releases: June 2nd, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
June 7th, 2017 (Wide) (Indonesia)
June 7th, 2017 (Wide) (Belgium)
June 7th, 2017 (Wide) (Netherlands)
June 7th, 2017 (Wide) (Philippines)
Video Release: August 22nd, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence, action and scary images, and for some suggestive content and partial nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2474 (Cert #51038), 5/3/2017)
Running Time: 107 minutes
Franchise: Dark Universe
Cast, crew, or production detail: Failed Franchises, Intertitle, Prologue, Reboot, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: England
Plot point: Dead Language, Hallucinations, Monster, Mummy, Non-Chronological, Revenge, Treasure Hunters, Extreme Weather
Subgenre: Chase Adventure
Time period setting: 1100s, Ancient Egypt
Source:Based on Folk Tale/Legend/Fairytale
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Perfect World Pictures, Secret Hideout, Conspiracy Factory, Sean Daniel Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
